commands.name: spec
commands.description: Generate code from a pattern spec. Usage: /spec <spec-id> [feature-name]
commands.prompt: Read the spec at specs/${1}.spec.md. Follow the AGENT.system-prompt.md contract exactly. Generate the full component scaffold for the feature named '${2}'. Output the compliance report first, then the generated files.

commands.name: spec-search
commands.description: Find a spec for a use case. Usage: /spec-search <description>
commands.prompt: The developer needs a spec for: '${1}'. Search the registry.json for matching specs by tag or title. List all matches with their spec-id, status, and a one-line description. If nothing matches, suggest the closest spec and explain the gap.

commands.name: spec-check
commands.description: Check if existing code complies with its spec. Usage: /spec-check <spec-id>
commands.prompt: Review the selected code against the spec at specs/${1}.spec.md. Run through the Agent Checklist in §11 of the spec. For each item, mark ✓ (compliant), ✗ (violation), or ⚠ (partial). List all violations with the exact line and what the spec requires.

commands.name: spec-new
commands.description: Scaffold a new spec from the template. Usage: /spec-new <spec-id> <title>
commands.prompt: Create a new spec file for spec-id '${1}' titled '${2}'. Use docs/SPEC.template.md as the base. Fill in the frontmatter fields (set status to 'draft', today's date for last-reviewed). Leave content sections as instructed placeholders. Save to specs/${1}.spec.md.

commands.name: spec-diff
commands.description: Show what changed between spec versions. Usage: /spec-diff <spec-id> <old-version> <new-version>
commands.prompt: Compare version ${2} and ${3} of the spec '${1}' using the change log in §12. Summarize what changed and flag any breaking changes that would require re-generation of existing components.
